quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Never set running text below 12px.

relations.note: Constrains the weight cuts available for the mandated families.
Applies the family/weight system to concrete email sizes.
Line-height minimum overlays the email type scale.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos**
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos

rules.intent: Enforce the approved font family per medium and level.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headers use Agenda (font.headline.print = Agenda) in print and Nunito Sans (font.body.primary = Nunito Sans) with bolder weights and Title Case in digital/email; Aptos is the Word/PPT/Excel fallback. Body copy, footers and fine print use Nunito Sans (Aptos fallback). Agenda may be used as live text in digital ONLY for the campaign headline (campaign_headline.font = Agenda).

quotes: Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

rules.intent: Constrain type weights to the licensed/available cuts.
rules.rule_text: Only the published weight sets may be used: Agenda — Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans — Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos — Regular, Bold.

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
**[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it

rules.intent: Guarantee consistent typography with graceful email-client fallback.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email CSS font stack is font.email.stack = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Preserve the approved email type scale and minimum sizes.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 (section hero headline) 28px/36px (≈130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 (section heading) 22px/30px (≈136%) Nunito Sans Bold; H3 (subhead) 18px/26px (≈144%) Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular — 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better"); Secondary/supporting body 14px/21px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular, use sparingly and never for primary content; Footnote/reference text 12px/18px (150%). Never set running text below 12px (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component).

quotes: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Meet the brand accessibility bar for legibility.
rules.rule_text: Line height is a minimum of 150% for all body copy (line_height.body.min = 150%) as a brand accessibility rule; for light text on dark backgrounds increase further — [GENERAL] use 160% (line_height.light_on_dark.160 = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Ensure typographic hierarchy communicates reading order.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
